findings.description: poll_loop is declared async but never awaits anything — it calls blocking time.sleep (line 79) and the fully-synchronous run_with_retries (line 81), so it monopolizes the event loop and stalls every other coroutine on the service's loop for the full duration of every job and every poll interval.

attempt is reset to 0 every iteration before the increment, so the while attempt < MAX_RETRIES loop never terminates on a persistently-failing job — the worker thread is pinned forever, sleeping ~2s per spin and never draining the queue (logic bug with severe resource-starvation impact; defer the correctness framing to critic-logic).
The intended exponential backoff is dead: because attempt is forced to 0 just above, the sleep is always RETRY_BACKOFF_SECONDS * (0+1) = a constant 2.0s and never grows across retries.
pop(0) on a list is O(n) because it shifts all remaining elements; draining N jobs via drain_sync/poll_loop is therefore O(n^2) — use collections.deque.popleft() for O(1) dequeue.
When the queue is empty this blocking time.sleep(POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS) freezes the entire event loop for a full second per empty poll; in an async context it must be await asyncio.sleep(...).

The 'convert' branch runs subprocess.run synchronously, blocking the calling thread (and, via poll_loop, the event loop) for the entire external process; should be offloaded to a thread/executor or asyncio subprocess — note shell=True is also a command-injection vector, deferred to critic-security.
